name: ABYSSAL OGRE
description: Underground. A towering sightless giant with translucent stone-cold skin; dwells in lightless depths, using immense pale limbs to crush intruders and drag them into crushing pressure.

name: ABYSSCROWN TUSK-WHALE
description: A larger, more scarred deep-sea Crowntooth with teeth blackened by volcanic vent deposits.

name: AEIRHYD
description: Neutral Sea Dragon of Warman’s Lake.

name: AETHERIUM GOLEM
description: Aether-aligned construct of white metal, crystal joints, and floating inner rings. Appears only partly present, with portions of its body phasing behind the world.

name: ALAMIR HARE
description: A long-eared plains hare prized for its speed and as a food source. Considered good luck to release one if caught alive.

name: AMETHYSTWING PACKRAPTOR
description: A Packraptor variant with violet wing-frills used for threat display and pack signaling. Hunts along cliff paths, scree slopes, and mineral-streaked terrain where its coloration blends with stone.

name: ANOMALOCARIS
description: A prehistoric aquatic apex predator featuring a segmented body, stalked eyes, and barbed grasping appendages used to snatch prey into its circular plate-lined mouth.

name: ANUBITAPH
description: A fierce bipedal jackal-like plains predator with long ears, lean muscle, and a hunter's cunning; feared for stalking the grasslands in disciplined packs. They use weapons like khopesh swords and long square shields.

name: APOPHIS
description: Devourer of Light; Titan Cyclops.

name: ARID OGRE
description: Desert. A towering suns-baked giant with cracked sandstone-like skin; can remain motionless for weeks amidst dunes, waiting to crush travelers who mistake its massive form for a weathered rock.

name: ASH SERPENT
description: A heat-resistant serpent that inhabits volcanic rock fields. Ambushes prey by emerging from ash drifts. Its scales resist fire damage. Venom causes rapid internal inflammation.

name: ASHBEAR
description: A broad-shouldered forest bear with ash-grey fur and a territorial temperament. Largely avoids settlements but increasingly displaced by Veinrunner expansion into old-growth timber regions.

name: ASHDUNE GRAVEBEAK
description: A lean, pale desert strain of the great hammer-billed flightless predator; can go long periods without water by feeding on blood and organ meat.

name: ASHEN REVENANT
description: A rare and dangerous undead formed when a highly spiritually significant individual dies in proximity to a collapsed Veyline. Retains memory, intent, and growing intelligence. Unlike standard undead, an Ashen Revenant can form goals. It can make plans. It remembers who it was and has decided what it wants to do about what happened to it. High to Campaign-level threat. The Scribe recommends treating any confirmed Ashen Revenant sighting as a campaign-level event.

name: AUROX
description: A broad-horned feral humanoid of plain and grove, known for calm strength.

name: AWAKENED JADE GARGOYLE
description: A carved jade guardian given predatory animation through corrupted resonance or failed warding rites. Unlike ordinary gargoyles, it remembers the place it was made to protect and may continue enforcing commands long after their meaning has been lost.

name: BARK SPIDER
description: A palm-sized arachnid that blends perfectly into tree bark. Bite causes localized swelling and mild fever. Not dangerous to healthy adults.

name: BASALT OGRE
description: Volcanic. A towering obsidian-skinned titan forged from cooled volcanic rock; body as hard as mountain stone, exudes intense smoldering heat from glowing magma fissures in its joints.

name: BEBELITH
description: A spider-like horror with cruel intelligence, hooked limbs, and a hunger for sanctified flesh. Most often found near desecrated shrines, abandoned temples, or places where protective rites failed. Covers its face with a set of grotesque hands; if it looks directly at a victim they are immediately stunned.

name: BEHIR
description: A blue-scaled serpentine monstrosity with lightning breath.

name: BLACKRIDGE DREAD-CROC
description: A monstrous crocodilian with jagged black dorsal ridges and a snout broader than a man is tall; becomes mud, log, shadow, and shoreline before lunging with violence that seems impossible for something so still.

name: BLACKWOOD APEX-URUS
description: A massive pursuit-predator bear of dense forest with dark greenish-grey fur and natural markings resembling broken script; moves through heavy timber with surprising quiet. Unlike ordinary bears, it runs prey down.

name: BLIGHTED
description: Any race corrupted by Dybuk influence, Void contamination, Maelificar shadow, or Apostate resonance.

name: BLINDPLATE MAW
description: A pale, eyeless armored cave fish guided by vibration; haunts flooded caverns and strikes at anything that disturbs the water.

name: BLUEWHORL SAWSPIRAL
description: The open-water strain of Sawspiral; slate-blue, follows wounded whales and fishing fleets.

name: BLUEWRITHE SERPENT-WHALE
description: The common open-sea strain; its calls travel through hulls like distant thunder.

name: BOGLURKER
description: A submerged predator that stays perfectly still beneath the muck; uses vine-like feelers to ensnare the legs of those crossing the water.

name: BONE CONSTRUCT
description: Deliberately assembled undead created through Magister or Necromancer intervention. Require an active will to maintain animation. Medium threat alone; campaign-level threat in organized groups under intelligent command.

name: BOUNTY CREW
description: Licensed or unlicensed individuals hunting a specific target. Professional, focused. Medium threat.

name: BRAMBLEHORN TITAN-HOPPER
description: A darker, shorter-limbed forest strain of Titan-Hopper; males grow small antler-like brow spurs used in mating clashes.

name: BRAMBLEHOUND
description: A disorienting woodland stalker that leads prey in endless looping trails, its shifting fur and misleading scent causing travelers to walk willingly into ambushes.

name: BRIARDREID
description: A massive root-bound predator that drags prey into the deeper timber.

name: BRINDLE CATTLE
description: A broad-framed semi-wild bovine of the grasslands, domesticated in most provinces but found feral along frontier roads. Their stampedes have derailed at least two Veinrunner freight loads in recorded history.

name: BRINDLECALF
description: A small sure-footed grazer of meadow and brush, known for its gentle nature.

name: BRINECROWN TUSK-WHALE
description: The surface-hunting strain; follows whale pods and fishing fleets, attacking where blood and noise gather.

name: BRINEPLATE MAW
description: A broad dark-blue ocean Ironplate Maw that patrols reefs and shipwrecks.

name: BRINESICKLE SCYTHE-SHRIMP
description: A blue-grey reef-channel Scythe-Shrimp that follows larger predators to feed on scraps.

name: BUMBLEBRUIN
description: A fuzzy bee-bear of meadow and grove, known for gathering nectar.

name: BURDEN GOLEM
description: Weight-aligned construct of lead, black granite, dense iron, and pressure-runes. The ground buckles under it even when it stands still, and enemies nearby feel their own bodies become heavier.

name: CAIRNWOLF
description: A mountain sentinel that lies motionless among stone piles and cairns, erupting into motion to trigger rockslides and crush isolated prey.

name: CANIVINE
description: A woodland hound wreathed in creeping vines and thorned jaws, known for silent stalking and sudden vicious lunges. Can creep into cities.

name: CANIVINE (URBAN)
description: The same woodland canivine that has followed rail and trade roads into city margins. More cautious than its woodland counterpart. No less dangerous.

name: CANOPY APE
description: A large, sociable primate of the upper jungle. Not aggressive unless young are threatened. Troops of thirty or more are common. Their alarm calls function as a reliable early warning system.

name: CANOPY CONSTRICTOR
description: A massive tree-dwelling serpent that drops onto prey from above. Non-venomous. Kills by constriction. Long enough to encircle a draft horse. Prefers large warm-blooded prey and has been known to target sleeping camp groups.

name: CANOPY RAVEN
description: A large glossy-black bird of the upper forest. Highly intelligent, mimics voices with unnerving accuracy, and is believed by some Pa'morph Kra'ark to be a distant cousin. Considered a trickster omen.

name: CANYON HAWK
description: A large territorial raptor of the badlands. Attacks anything that enters its nesting zone without discrimination. Nests are almost impossible to spot from below. A serious hazard to solo travelers.

name: CAP RAUK
description: Fungal Forest. A mushroom-plumed fungal rauk that slips soundlessly through dim groves and cavern roots; often taken as an omen of damp rot and hidden growth.

name: CARAGAR
description: Chaotic Evil Fire Wyrm of Lilith’iel.

name: CARRION KITE
description: A large bird of prey that feeds on carrion. Often seen circling above battlefields, waiting for its next meal.

name: CARRION WINGED KITE
description: A larger variant of the Carrion Kite with broader wings, allowing it to soar higher and cover more ground in search of food. Often arrives at battlefields before standard Carrion Kites and departs last.

name: CARRION-SAIL CLOUD-VULTURE
description: A vast gas-bladdered battlefield scavenger whose shadow over a road is considered a bad omen by soldiers; its presence in numbers means something has already died nearby or soon will.

name: CARRIONZELLE
description: A wasteland-dwelling, carrion-feeding gazelle-like creature with ragged horns, blackened hooves, and a taste for battlefield remains. Skittish when alone, but dangerous in numbers.

name: CAVE BEAR
description: A massive bear adapted to mountain cave systems. Hibernates during cold seasons. Much larger than the ashbear. Encounters near cave entrances in early spring are extremely dangerous.

name: CAVE FISH
description: Blind, pale fish found in underground waterways. Primary food source in long underground expeditions. Nutritious and surprisingly easy to catch in still water.

name: CAVE MOTH
description: A large pale moth that navigates cavern systems by echolocation. Non-aggressive. Their wing dust causes mild irritation to eyes and airways but is not toxic.

name: CAVERN SPIDER
description: A blind cave-adapted spider with a body the size of a large dog. Hunts by vibration. Webs are nearly invisible in darkness and strong enough to hold a struggling human.

name: CAVERNOUS GHOSTCRAB
description: A blind, pale ghostcrab that inhabits underground streams and flooded cavern shelves. Navigates by vibration and retreats into cracks too narrow for most predators to follow.

name: CEDAR OGRE
description: Woodland. A towering bark-skinned giant wreathed in living cedar boughs; sentinel of the ancient woods, crushing those who threaten the sanctity of the grove.

name: CENTAUR
description: A fierce plains hunter whose speed and pride make it dangerous to cross.

name: CERULEN
description: Neutral Sea Dragon of the Azure Tides.

name: CHAINWAKE SERPENT-WHALE
description: An island-channel Longwrithe that travels predictable routes; island pilots learn its passages the way farmers learn river floods.

name: CHRONOVORE
description: A time-devouring hunter of the Chronison that consumes stolen years, alternate selves, and forbidden futures.

name: CINDER GOLEM
description: Fire-aligned construct of kiln-stone, iron ribs, and furnace-glass veins. Body smolders constantly, shedding ember flakes as it moves; damaged plates expose molten heat beneath the frame.

name: CITY CROW
description: The urban equivalent of the canopy raven. Highly intelligent, adaptable, and present in every major Soterian city. Known to steal small objects. Some individual city crows have been observed following specific people for days, apparently by choice.

name: CLAWSTRUTH RAPTOR-STRUTHIO
description: A feathered pursuit predator with a retractable killing claw; sprints in terrifying bursts, slashes, retreats, circles, and repeats until prey collapses. Packs can bring down riders and livestock.

name: CLIFF ADDER
description: A venomous mountain snake that basks on sun-facing rock faces. Camouflaged against stone. Venom causes rapid cardiac stress in small creatures.

name: CLOUDSCYTHE MANTIS
description: A high-altitude mantis predator with pale cloud-colored plating and long hooked forelimbs. Clings to cliff faces and mist-wrapped trees, striking from concealment before vanishing into fog.

name: CLOUDWHALE
description: A vast, gentle aerial creature that drifts at extreme altitude feeding on atmospheric particulates. Rarely seen clearly. Considered a blessing omen by most Soterian cultures. Their passing is accompanied by a low harmonic resonance that spiritually sensitive individuals can perceive as immense calm. - CLOUDRAY - A large, manta ray-like creature that glides through the sky on broad membranous wings. Feeds on airborne plankton and small birds. Non-aggressive and often seen in groups, their graceful flight is considered a symbol of freedom and tranquility. - FAE CLOUDWHALE - A smaller, more colorful variant of the Cloudwhale, adorned with iridescent patterns on its skin. Known for its playful nature and tendency to interact with other flying creatures, it is often seen as a symbol of joy and whimsy in Soterian folklore.

name: COBALT RAUK
description: Grassland. A blue-plumed plains rauk.

name: COGDOG
description: A grimforge industrial hound with exposed gears and pistons beneath patchy fur, patrolling rail yards and factories with a clicking, wheezing gait.

name: COMMON FISH
description: Perch, carp, and river trout equivalents found in most Soterian waterways. Primary freshwater food source.

name: CORDYCEP HOUND
description: A wolf-sized creature overtaken entirely by a parasitic fungal organism. The original animal is dead; the fungus animates the body with surprising tactical coordination. Attacks in silence. Produces spores on death that can begin the same process in living hosts over several days.

name: CRADLECRAG BOULDER-WOMBAT
description: A mountain strain of Boulder-Wombat with thicker hide and stone-dusted shoulder plates; often mistaken for fallen boulders during fog.

name: CRAG RAUK
description: Cavernous. A sharp-beaked ash-colored rauk that dwells in lightless cavern hollows; navigates through densest stalactite forests by emitting a low-frequency hum that echoes off stone.

name: CRAG-RUNE APEX-URUS
description: A mountain strain of the great Runebear with dark brown or slate fur and heavier forelimbs for climbing broken slopes; notorious for appearing above travelers rather than behind them.

name: CRAGSPOT SKY-NECK
description: A colossal cliff-nesting pterosaur with a giraffe-long neck and mottled grey-rust hide; uses its reach to pluck prey from ledges and ruins without committing its full body to danger.

name: CRAGWING RAZORGALE
description: A lean mountain pterosaur specialized in knocking prey from ledges rather than carrying them away; nests in sheer cliff cracks and is nearly invisible against stone.

name: CRIMSON RAUK
description: Woodland/Highland. A scarlet-plumed highland rauk that rides the wind like an omen, known for its piercing cry and watchful circles above the forest trees.

name: CROWNTOOTH TUSK-WHALE
description: A predatory whale armed with massive crown-like ivory fangs in both jaws; actively hunts giant squid, lesser whales, sea serpents, and ships that resemble wounded beasts.

name: CRYOGRE
description: Tundra. A massive frost-rimed titan whose skin is a patchwork of permafrost and jagged ice; can freeze the very air with its breath before shattering paralyzed prey with a single earth-shaking blow.

name: CRYPTID
description: A skulking woodland creature known for unnatural cunning, sudden violence, and half-glimpsed shapes between the trees.

name: CULT ENFORCERS
description: Armed operatives of any of Soteria's active cult structures (the Verdant Spiral, the Harrow-adjacent practitioners, the Stonecutter Clan, etc.). True believers are more dangerous than mercenaries. Medium-to-High threat.

name: DART FROG (SOTERIAN)
description: A brilliantly colored small amphibian whose skin secretes a potent contact toxin. Collected by Brunar traders for poison applications. Handling without protection is strongly inadvisable.

name: DEEP CRAWLER
description: A multi-legged pale predator of the lightless underground. Hunts by vibration detection. Extremely sensitive to sound; loud noises cause disorientation. Produces a faint bioluminescent slime trail. Medium threat in confined tunnels.

name: DEEP WHALE
description: A massive cetacean of the open ocean. Non-aggressive. Their resonance signatures are among the most stable natural phenomena in Soteria. Wyth scholars believe deep whales function as passive anchors for oceanic Veyline stability.

name: DEEPPLATE ARMOR-CRAWLER
description: A blind, pale-bellied cave strain of Platewyrme with dark-above, chalky-beneath plating; silent on stone and devastating in confined tunnels.

name: DEPOT MOUSE
description: A clean, surprisingly healthy rodent variant found exclusively in Veinrunner depot grain stores. Has developed immunity to most standard rodent deterrents. The depot workers have largely given up and named several of them.

name: DESERT GHOSTCRAB
description: A sand-pale ghostcrab that burrows beneath dune crusts and dry salt flats. Emerges at night to scavenge insects, lizards, and carrion.

name: DESERT LIZARD
description: A common agile lizard of dry regions. Edible. Considered a survival food staple. Some species are mildly venomous.

name: DESERT TORTOISE
description: A slow-moving armored reptile of arid regions. Extremely long-lived. Some individuals are believed to carry resonance impressions from centuries past. Considered sacred in certain Ahnkir traditions. Harming one is considered deeply poor judgment.

name: DIAMOND RAUK
description: Arctic. A crystalline translucent-plumed rauk that refracts the light of the three suns into dazzling prisms; a brilliant yet cold omen above frozen wastes.

name: DOCK BIRD
description: Common large seabird of port districts. Aggressive around food but not genuinely dangerous. Their alarm calls have alerted more than one dockside guard to intruders.

name: DRAEKUL
description: A corrupted Drakazir bloodline driven by hoard-lust, bloodfire, ancient tyranny, and draconic supremacy.

name: DRAUGR
description: A grave-bound undead or Rootwraught horror associated with burial sites, cold memory, and violent refusal to remain dead. Often found near old barrows, battlefield graves, frozen ruins, and places where the dead were not properly witnessed.

name: DROWNED
description: A specific Echoing undead type found near significant water features where drowning deaths have occurred in numbers. Semi-aquatic. Slower on land. Extremely dangerous in water. Resonance: Echoing.

name: DRYT
description: A shy woodland spirit that tends roots, streams, and wounded trees.

name: DUERGAR
description: A deep-forge Durinak counterlineage hardened by buried spite, cruel industry, and the black pressure of the underworld.

name: DUNECLAW RAPTOR-STRUTHIO
description: A pale-feathered desert pursuit predator that hunts at dawn and dusk when shadows make its sprint harder to read.

name: DUNEFLASH SUNSAIL
description: A desert strain of Sunsail Skink with orange, rust, and gold sail markings; buries itself under sand at night and emerges at dawn.

name: DUNEHEEL TITAN-HOPPER
description: A pale-furred desert strain of Titan-Hopper with wide heat-shedding ears and an efficient stride capable of crossing salt flats that kill horses.

name: DUNESTRIDE SKY-NECK
description: A pale-hide narrow-winged desert pterosaur with a heat-shedding throat pouch; stalks dry washes and ancient roadways waiting for exhausted creatures to slow beneath its shadow.

name: DUNEWOLF
description: A desert pack hunter whose sand-colored fur and flattened silhouette make it nearly invisible until it erupts from dunes in a storm of teeth and dust.

name: DUNSHRIKE
description: A lean, long-legged plains grazer.

name: DUSTMOUND BOULDER-WOMBAT
description: A plains strain that digs wide, shallow tunnel networks; disliked by ranchers whose livestock break legs in old burrow roofs, but valued by farmers for soil-turning.

name: DUSTRAT
description: A small scrubland rodent with quick paws and sharp instincts; known for burrowing beneath dry earth.

name: DYBUK
description: A shadow-born entity of shifting form that feeds on fear, grief, and despair; grows more real and dangerous wherever suffering lingers. Nature: Born from the Void (Tophet). No physical bodies; they require a host. Drawn to individuals in states of spiritual vulnerability: grief, humiliation, envy, failure, obsession, despair, broken oaths, abandoned faith, or lost Veyline alignment. On Nazros their feeding behavior is industrialized (Operation Trust). In Soteria, they operate individually or in small clusters. Conditions increasing manifestation risk: mass grief, betrayal, unresolved trauma, isolation, false Nullite exposure, collapsed faith structures, prolonged silence, abandoned settlements, and corrupted Veylines. Conditions reducing manifestation risk: genuine communal trust, active Sanctus practice, truthful witness, functioning Veylines, emotionally stable populations, and dawn-aligned ritual activity. Feeding: Dybuks feed on live despair — not death itself, but the sustained experience of loss while still alive. A Dybuk will often keep a host functional while harvesting suffering rather than simply killing them. Possession signs: Discoloration of veins, coldness of skin, eyes that reflect light incorrectly, uncharacteristic calm followed by sudden violence. Weaknesses: True spiritual alignment, Sanctus discipline, Caelyn pact-bonds, genuine Nullite, sunlight during Ba'el (Dawn pass). Strengths: Isolation, silence, false Nullite, broken oaths, grieving spaces. Threat Level: Medium solo / High when possessing a powerful or named NPC / Campaign-level when organized. Combat behavior: A Dybuk fights using the host's memories, abilities, and relationships — it will exploit all of them. It targets witnesses and allies first to isolate the party. Avoids direct confrontation with Divine-aligned characters. If the host body is destroyed, the Dybuk is expelled but not destroyed — it seeks a new host immediately. True destruction requires a Caelyn severing the Veyline connection, a Sanctus exorcism, or a binding ritual returning it to Tophet.

name: DYBUK-HOST (LATE STAGE)
description: When a Dybuk has occupied a host long enough, the host body begins to physically change. Veins blacken visibly. Temperature drops measurably. The host's original personality is effectively consumed. The host cannot be saved through conventional means. Resonance: Void-Tainted. High threat.

name: ECHOING DEAD
description: A rare form of undead in which the deceased's resonance impression is so strong that it persists as a semi-autonomous presence. Often found at sites of tremendous emotional significance — last stands, executions, places of profound betrayal. They remember who they were, and that memory makes them unpredictable. High threat. Resonance: Echoing.

name: EMRYS
description: Neutral Good Imperial Dragon of Verdelie’re.

name: FAECOLT
description: A small enchanted horse of glade and grove, known for its speed and gentle temperament.

name: FEN OGRE
description: Marsh. A bloated water-logged giant that generates a thick sulfurous miasma from its skin, blinding players while it hunts by vibration.

name: FERNPUP
description: A soft-coated forest pup known for nesting in fernbeds and befriending lost travelers.

name: FERRABORNE
description: A hard-bodied Pa’morph opposite shaped by iron, invasive growth, and anti-natural adaptation rather than living animal harmony.

name: FERRUGWORATH
description: A rust-red gorilla creature that seeks out metal and leaves only corrosion behind. Increasingly seen near Veinrunner rail lines where exposed iron is abundant.

name: FERRUGWORATH (RAIL VARIANT)
description: The standard Ferrugworath adapted to follow the Veinrunner rail network, attracted by the concentration of exposed iron and steel. Rail variant specimens are larger and more aggressive than plains counterparts. The Veinrunner Authority has an open bounty.

name: FETCH DIREBEAK
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a large predatory bird, able to appear as crow, raven, hawk, owl, kite, or vulture depending on the druid’s bond. Used for scouting, message-carrying, aerial harassment, and warning calls.

name: FETCH DIREBOAR
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a heavy tusked boar with dense shoulders and stubborn spirit-force. It charges through brush, breaks weak barricades, and defends its summoner with brutal direct aggression.

name: FETCH DIREDRAKE
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a small dire drake with rugged scales, clawed limbs, and a low predatory stance. Used for guarding, intimidation, and short-range combat, it fights with snapping jaws and sudden lunges.

name: FETCH DIRELIZARD
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a rugged dire lizard with clawed feet, a thick tail, and alert reptilian senses. It clings to stone, darts through undergrowth, and fights with quick bites, tail sweeps, and stubborn survival instinct. Ogres are believed to be ancient adaptive megafauna shaped directly by environmental resonance pressure. Unlike ordinary creatures, Ogres exhibit terrain mimicry, environmental synchronization, abnormal durability, and ecosystem-level territorial behavior. Some scholars argue Ogres represent "the same primordial organism repeatedly rewritten by different regions of Soteria." OGRE FAMILY — BIOME VARIANTS:

name: FETCH DIREPARD
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a large predatory cat, ranging from lynxlike to pantherlike depending on the summoner’s region. Silent, agile, and precise, it excels at stalking, ambush, climbing, and striking exposed targets.

name: FETCH DIRERYU
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a floating serpentine ryū, called from wind, mist, leaf-spirits, or old river resonance. Graceful and watchful, it scouts from above, coils through the air, and lashes at threats with a spectral bite or tail-strike.

name: FETCH DIRETOAD
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like an oversized swamp toad with slick hide, springing legs, and a wide gripping mouth. It leaps suddenly, clings to enemies, and can harry foes with croaks, slime, or minor toxic secretions.

name: FETCH DIREWARG
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a twisted grim-warg with a low body, heavy jaws, and cruel hunting instincts. More aggressive than the Direwolf Fetch, it excels at pursuit, fear tactics, and dragging enemies off balance.

name: FETCH DIREWOLF
description: A druidic familiar summon shaped like a larger-than-natural wolf with spirit-bright eyes and bristling fur. Loyal, fast, and tactically aware, it tracks enemies, guards camps, and helps bring down prey through coordinated bites.

name: FIELD CROW
description: Common black bird of open country. Highly adaptable, found near settlements, farms, and rail depots. Considered a pest in most agricultural provinces.

name: FLAKE RAUK
description: Tundra. A pale downy-feathered rauk that blends perfectly into falling snow; a silent omen of approaching frost or a guide for those lost in blizzard white.

name: FOG-O-WAR
description: A jellyfish-like cicada horror that gathers where fear, smoke, and unresolved violence linger. It manifests as a low crawling fog filled with half-seen limbs, muffled commands, and sudden grasping shapes.

name: FOREST ADDER
description: A banded venomous snake of the undergrowth. Non-aggressive unless disturbed. Venom causes mild paralysis of the affected limb.

name: FOREST GHOSTCRAB
description: A moss-backed ghostcrab found in damp hollows, old wells, and rain-swollen root systems far from the sea. Scribes believe its ancestors followed underground waterways inland and never returned.

name: FRESHWATER TURTLE
description: Common large turtle of river banks and lake edges. Docile. Edible. Their shells are used in a variety of craft applications.

name: FROSTMANE SPIRALHORN
description: A massive tundra-adapted beast with a huge spiral horn that bores into frozen ground and bears a crown of ice crystals after feeding; its charge twists rather than simply impales.

name: FROSTSHELL GLACIER-TURTLE
description: A colossal ancient sea turtle carrying ice, pale barnacles, and frost algae across its shell; sleeping specimens are mistaken for small ice floes in cold seas.

name: FROSTWRAITH
description: A spectral ice-veined predator that manifests from killing cold; haunts frozen wastes with a chilling touch and silent predatory hunger for the warmth of the living. Related to but distinct from the Whispering Frostwraith of the Falorn Fjords, which is near-Titan scale.

name: FURBACK EURYPTI
description: A broad, many-legged subterranean predator with coarse fur along its plated back and grasping forelimbs adapted for tight tunnels. Waits half-buried in loose stone, then surges forward to seize prey and drag it into narrow cracks.

name: GALE-RIB GOLEM
description: Air-aligned construct of light wood, silver wire, sailcloth, and hollow bone. Much of its body is invisible pressure and compressed wind, making its strikes difficult to read.

name: GARGOYLE
description: A stone-winged city predator that lurks upon rooftops and spires; strikes intruders with sudden claws and brutal silence.

name: GASBLOOM CLOUD-VULTURE
description: A vast floating scavenger with a gas-filled throat pouch that allows near-wingless drifting over battlefields; releases a rotten flammable belch when startled, strong enough to sicken horses.

name: GENIE
description: A dangerous true wish-being whose bargains bend desire into debt, servitude, elemental punishment, or ruin.

name: GLACIAL GHOSTCRAB
description: A translucent ice-shelled crab that nests in cracks beneath glaciers and frozen coastal shelves. Its body is nearly invisible against blue ice, and its claws can numb exposed flesh with prolonged contact.

name: GLASS PHARAOH
description: A Titan-Shed construct of fused desert glass and ancient bone that believes itself the ruler of all sand. It "taxes" travelers by extracting water and memories.

name: GLASSWING STEELNEEDLE
description: A translucent-winged coastal strain that nests near tide pools; feeds on seabirds, crabs, and stranded fish before turning on larger prey.

name: GLIMROOT
description: A sentient glowing root-system that entangles intruders.

name: GLOAMWING
description: A large bat with wings that shimmer like the twilight sky. Nocturnal, feeds on insects and small mammals.

name: GLOOMWHORL SAWSPIRAL
description: A dark, slow, nearly blind deep-sea Sawspiral with pale sensory pits; its tooth-whorl may glow faintly from deep-sea organisms caught between the blades.

name: GLOW WORM
description: A small bioluminescent larva found in cave ceilings in large colonies. Their combined light is sufficient to navigate by. Harvested and used in small lantern capsules by underground workers.

name: GLOWCAP
description: A soft bioluminescent fungus-creature that gathers in damp groves and cavern roots.

name: GLOWHORN PALEHIDE
description: A subterranean drill-rhino strain with pale skin, poor eyesight, and a faintly bioluminescent horn; when the hornlight appears in a tunnel, the safest path is usually backward.

name: GOBLIN
description: Small cunning humanoids known for scavenging, ambush tactics, and crude ingenuity. Goblin tribes dwell in hidden woodland dens, abandoned ruins, tunnel systems, and scrap settlements near older trade roads. Though physically weak individually, they become dangerous in coordinated numbers.

name: GOLDGRASS GRAVEBEAK
description: A towering flightless predator with a sledgehammer beak and a curved killing point; the classic man-killer strain of open savannah, often seen as a dark shape cutting through high grass before the head drops like a smith's hammer.

name: GOLDPLUME CLAWSTRUTH
description: A feathered ostrich-like predator with a retractable killing claw on each foot; hunts in loose packs using dust clouds to confuse prey, fast enough to overtake a horse over short ground.

name: GOLDSTEP TITAN-HOPPER
description: The most common strain of Titan-Hopper, featuring wheat-colored fur and strong migratory instincts. Its herds move like living weather across open country.

name: GORJACK
description: A brutal grassland hunter that bounds through thorn and reed, notorious for dragging down larger prey with hooked jaws.

name: GRAFTWRIGHT
description: A predatory falsework creature that grafts itself into objects, rooms, and structures, taking the form of what victims expect to find before latching, swallowing, or absorbing them. Often mistaken for chests, doors, shrines, books, tools, wagons, or relics. Older specimens can become part of an entire room.

name: GRANULE PACKRAPTOR
description: A dust-colored Packraptor with rough pebble-like scales along its spine and shoulders. Often rolls through loose soil to mask its scent before ambushing prey.

name: GRASSLAND ADDER
description: A thick-bodied venomous snake that basks on sun-warmed stones and road surfaces. Responsible for more traveler injuries than any predator in the province records.

name: GRAVEBEAK HAMMER-BIRD
description: A towering flightless predator with a sledgehammer beak and a curved killing point; measures its target before the head drops like a smith's hammer. Nests are shallow ground-scrapes lined with bones, hide scraps, and bright stones.

name: GRAVEMASS GOLEM
description: Earth-aligned construct of packed clay, stone, root, bone fragments, and buried metal. Moves like a walking hill torn from an old battlefield, reshaping terrain as it advances.

name: GREATER DESERT SCORPION
description: A man-sized scorpion of rocky desert terrain. Venom causes paralysis within minutes. Hunts at night. During the day they shelter beneath rock overhangs and in ruins; the same places travelers seek shade.

name: GREATER GRAFTWRIGHT
description: A more powerful version of the Graftwright with stronger ability to integrate into and control its surroundings. Often found in more complex or elaborate structures.

name: GREATER JACK PACKRAPTOR
description: A larger and more formidable Jack Packraptor variant with thicker hide, heavier jaws, and dominant pack behavior. Often serves as leader among smaller Jack packs.

name: GREATER PACKRAPTOR
description: A large alpha-class Packraptor capable of leading mixed packs across plains, scrublands, and badland edges. Heavier frame makes it less agile than smaller variants but far more dangerous once it closes distance. 16. WASTES (CHRONOLITHE & CHEMICAL)

name: GREENMANE MAWLOAM
description: A common woodland strain of Mawloam armored in wiry fur layered with moss, lichen, and burrs; elder specimens may be mistaken for hillocks until their heads lift from undergrowth.

name: GREY RAUK
description: Badlands. A medium-sized mottled grey-feathered rauk with a heavy dust-coated beak; perches on barren rock within vast parched canyons.

name: GREY WOLF (SOTERIAN)
description: The common wolf of Soteria's forests and hills. Larger than expected, pack-coordinated, and known to avoid Dybuk-touched territory entirely. Hunters read wolf absence as a spiritual warning. Pack sizes range from four to sixteen.

name: GRID WRAITH
description: A Glyphic resonance entity that has formed around a damaged or overloaded Grid conduit node. Manifests as visible electrical discharge with apparent will and targeting behavior. Attacks anything conducting electricity or resonance. Cannot be negotiated with. Disrupted by grounding and genuine Nullite. Resonance: Glyphic.

name: GRIMRITE BEETLE
description: A large subterranean insect whose carapace has partially crystallized from generations of grimrite exposure. Mandibles can penetrate standard leather armor. Found in collapsed mine sections and near raw grimrite veins. Resonance: Grimrite-Saturated.

name: GRIMRITE POACHERS
description: Illegal grimrite extraction crews. Armed. Paranoid. Willing to use violence to protect their operation. Medium threat.

name: GRIMRITE WOLF
description: A grey wolf pack that has inhabited a mine collapse zone long enough for grimrite saturation to partially crystallize their skeletal structure through their hides. Pack-hunting behavior is unchanged. Their bites can cause minor resonance contamination in open wounds. Resonance: Grimrite-Saturated. Found near the Corren Mountain Mines and the older Durinak extraction sites.

name: GRINCOIL
description: A brightly marked arboreal serpent with a pale clown-like face, crimson crown-scales, and a wide painted-looking mouth resembling a cruel grin. Coils around trunks and branches, swaying like a performer before striking. Venom causes disorientation, laughter-like spasms, vivid false colors, and eventual paralysis.

name: GROTHMOGG
description: A hulking Othrod war-brute shaped by conquest, hunger, tribal cruelty, and relentless battlefield dominance.

name: GROVE PRISM-WING
description: A green, gold, and blue forest strain of Prism-Wing whose song mimics woodland birds and running water, making it nearly impossible to locate by ear.

name: GROVEKEEPER
description: Sylvan Grove. A large, solemn forest bird regarded as a silent overseer of the deep groves, known for its piercing cry and uncanny awareness of disturbances within ancient woodland regions. Repeated Grovekeeper calls are a warning of imbalance, blight, or nearby predatory movement.

name: GRYPHON
description: A proud winged hunter of cliff and sky, known for keen sight and fierce territorial wrath.

name: GULLY OGRE
description: Jungle. A moss-shrouded giant that lurks in deep ravines and jungle floor depressions, waiting for the Rhinallon to flush prey into its waiting reach.

name: GUST RAUK
description: Sky. A slender silver-plumed rauk that rides volatile wind currents of the high sky; harbinger of approaching storms or sudden violent gales.

name: GWOWRATH
description: A hulking grassland predator of brutal strength and bestial cunning, feared for savage charges, crushing limbs, and wild fury.

name: GWYLLION
description: A ragged crone appearing as an old woman who misleads travelers with echoing cries, yet may seek hearth and shelter when treated kindly.

name: HAEROBURROW
description: A burrowing insectoid predator that tunnels beneath loose soil, rotten roots, or clothing. Can be mistaken for a small plant prickle. Its arrival is often heard before it is seen: a dry scraping beneath the skin. Causes infestations that can last for months before eradicated. Also found in industrial waste zones near processing facilities.

name: HARROW PRACTITIONERS
description: Wielders of blood-adjacent or corruption-adjacent magic. Variable threat depending on training. Some are desperate individuals who made bad decisions. Others are deliberate architects of harm.

name: HEARTHWOOL SHAGGYSWINE
description: Extremely thick white or cream wool, often kept in cold settlements as a living bedwarmer. Its wool sheds in heavy seasonal clumps and is woven into mitts, boot-liners, and infant blankets.

name: HEIRESS NAGA
description: A regal serpentine lure-predator with a humanlike upper body and a great constrictor's tail. Drapes itself across branches, ruined balconies, and river stones like nobility at rest, feigning weakness until prey approaches. Its voice carries a soft commanding resonance that makes victims feel honored before the coils tighten.

name: HETH
description: Mount of Death; Titan Dracolith.

name: HIDE OGRE
description: Badlands. A lean hunched giant draped in a grisly patchwork of cured leathers and trophies; possesses supernatural ability to stitch itself into the environment, remaining invisible until it unfurls its heavy skin-bound club for a killing blow.

name: HIGHDRIFT CLOUD-VULTURE
description: A high-altitude mountain Cloud-Vulture that rides thin air above glaciers and cliffs, waiting for avalanches, falls, or failed climbs.

name: HIGHGLINT PRISM-WING
description: A silver, violet, and pale rose mountain strain of Prism-Wing whose feathers shine brightest in thin mountain air and dawn frost.

name: HOARFELL MAWLOAM
description: A pale, frost-furred northern strain of Mawloam with blue-edged teeth from mineral staining; moves silently through snow and vanishes into blizzard banks. Villages warn children never to approach a snow mound with breath.

name: HOLLOW
description: A Risen occupied by a minor Void fragment rather than a full Dybuk. More coordinated than standard Risen. Retains basic spatial awareness and primitive targeting. Does not feel pain. Eyes reflect light incorrectly. Resonance: Void-Tainted.

name: HOLLOW GOLEM
description: Void-aligned construct of black stone, dead glass, and negative space. Parts of its body appear missing, yet those absences strike with terrible force.

name: HOLLOWBEAR
description: An ashbear whose interior has been partially consumed by Draethic resonance from a collapsed Veyline. The cavity in its chest glows faintly in darkness. Attacks driven by pain rather than hunger. Resonance: Draethic.

name: HOLLOWJAW MARROWCRUNCHER
description: A cave strain of Bone-Cruncher with dark fur, enlarged ears, and a damp echoing call; cave explorers fear its silence more than its cry.

name: HOMUNCULUS
description: A small artificially created entity of uncertain origin, occasionally found operating independently in city environments. Intelligence varies widely. The Wyth have not reached consensus on whether they are creatures, constructs, or something else.

name: HORNBILL RAUK
description: Savannah. A broad-billed savannah rauk with dark plumage and a harsh cry; often seen as a fierce omen along roads, courts, and caravan trails.

name: HUSH
description: A radiant woodland spirit seen drifting through deep forest as a quiet guide for the lost, wounded, or worthy. It appears as a small winged humanoid made of soft golden-white light, its body half-formed and weightless, with mothlike or fae-like wings that shimmer like dusted glass. A Hush does not speak aloud; it beckons with gesture, warmth, and the faint pull of calm in the chest. Travelers who follow one often find safe paths, clean water, hidden clearings, or the way out of enchanted woods. To chase, trap, or shout at a Hush is considered ill fortune, for it vanishes from harsh intent and leaves only darkness behind.

name: HYR'DELEAUR
description: A small reptilian creature that feeds on dark emotions rather than flesh or plants.

name: ICE SEAL
description: A large marine mammal of arctic coasts. Docile and curious. Primary food source for arctic communities. Their fat provides fuel, their hide provides waterproofing, and their presence indicates open water nearby.

name: ICEWRITHE SERPENT-WHALE
description: A pale-scarred cold-water massive sea-serpent; surfaces among ice floes and is often mistaken for a moving ice ridge. When Icewrithes pass, smart captains stay ashore.

name: IFOR
description: Lawful Evil Storm Dragon of Zallakan.

name: IJIRIKI
description: A large moose-like beast with ornate branching antlers that resemble carved ivory and old rootwork. Generally peaceful, but territorial during rutting season; its antlers are prized in ceremonial carving and dangerous enough to break shields.

name: IMP
description: A small, sharp-featured creature with clever hands and a shrewd secretive nature; often drawn to hidden things and old bargains.

name: IRON RAT
description: A tunnel rat exposed to Grimrite runoff in city sewer systems. Larger than normal. Partially mineral-plated. Pack behavior. Resonance: Grimrite-Saturated.

name: IRONPLATE MAW
description: A heavily armored fish with bony skull plating and sharpened jawbone blades instead of teeth; rushes, bites, and rams through dock planks and boat hulls.

name: IRONWOOD DEER
description: A large stag of the deep forest whose antlers have a faint metallic sheen from grazing near grimrite-adjacent soil. Non-aggressive but capable of dangerous charges when cornered. Primary prey animal for woodland predators.

name: ISLEBACK GLACIER-TURTLE
description: A legendary Shellglacier Turtle large enough for seabirds to nest on its shell during long migrations.

name: ITHACUHS
description: A towering shaggy megafaunal beast resembling a fusion of mammoth and bear. Generally passive, but territorial males become violently aggressive during mating periods and drought migration.

name: IVORY PACKRAPTOR
description: A pale coldland Packraptor with ivory scales, low posture, and silent pack movement across snow and frozen scrub. Hunting strategy relies on patience, flanking, and driving prey into exhaustion.

name: JACK PACKRAPTOR
description: A small, agile pack predator known for coordinated attacks, short bounding leaps, hooked claws, and ferocious mandibles. Though individually manageable, a full pack can bring down prey many times its size through harassment, flanking, and relentless teamwork.

name: JADE PACKRAPTOR
description: A green-scaled Packraptor adapted to highland mountains, wet passes, and dense vegetation. Uses cover and angled terrain to break prey away from groups.

name: JADE RAUK
description: Jungle/Rainforest. A green-plumed jungle rauk that glides between canopy shadows; seen as an omen where the rainforest stills beneath its passing.

name: JEWEL-BACK SUNSAIL SKINK
description: Brightly colored with emerald, violet, or red sail patterns. Island children consider spotting one before a voyage to be a sign of good luck.

name: JUNGLE RAT
description: A large, adaptable rodent found throughout rainforest settlements and river camps. Disease vector. Responsible for several documented supply contamination incidents.

name: KHAASTA
description: Agile alligator-like reptilian predators with leathery hides; haunt mountainous regions using cunning and pack-hunting tactics to ambush prey along jagged cliffs.

name: KHELL SCHISMFORM
description: A rare Khell expression created when one Khellnid receives conflicting caste signals, producing a divided body, fractured instincts, or unstable allegiance. They do not survive in the hive long as they are culled.

name: KHELL UNSET
description: A Khellnid that has failed to caste-set and remains in a dangerous transitional larval state, absorbing traits from its surroundings without becoming a stable branch.

name: KHELL’XEN
description: A nightmare hive-predator Khell caste born from darkness, corpse-hives, toxic chambers, and predatory hosts. Develops black carapaces, climbing limbs, corrosive blood, and a merciless infestation instinct.

name: KHELLCHIN
description: A crustacean-insectoid Khell labor caste shaped by salt flats, marsh protein, shell-matter, and industrial contamination. Often exploited as workers, refugees, or quarantine populations, but dangerous when cornered.

name: KHELLHANAE
description: A massive brute Khell caste formed through heavy host mass, blood-fed war pits, and dominance pressure. Known for immense strength, pack hierarchy, trophies, siege violence, and command through brutality.

name: KHELLJTA
description: A solitary apex-hunter Khell caste formed through predatory host matter and isolation rites. Known for stalking worthy prey, wearing kill-masks, collecting trophies, and treating the hunt as sacred law.

name: KHELLNID
description: The blind larval root-form of all Khellkind. Pale, hook-mouthed parasites that read host tissue, memory residue, mineral exposure, and environmental pressure before caste-setting into a mature Khell branch.

name: KHELLOCH
description: A colony-form Khell caste in which many Khellnid bodies mature into coordinated worm-masses capable of occupying armor shells, dead giants, stone hollows, or siege frames.

name: KHELLSARII
description: A tall honor-bound Khell warrior caste with backward skull crests, split jawplates, raptorlike legs, and a rigid culture of duel-law, scar-honor, and oath-bound martial houses.

name: KHELLSKINI
description: A four armed Khell salvage caste shaped by machine ruins, Grid residue, metallic nutrient exposure, and wreckfield survival. Known for scavenging, engineering, raiding, and weaponizing broken technology.

name: KHELLYUUM
description: A rare priestly Khell caste cultivated in sacred nutrient pools, relic chambers, and ritual wombs. Physically delicate but mentally dangerous, serving as prophets, theologians, manipulators, and brood-rulers.

name: KILN MASTIFF
description: A volcanic brute with smoldering hide and forge-glowing jaws that ignites its surroundings, hunting amid ash flows and leaving trails of blackened glass.

name: KRAG
description: Small, agile, winged creatures with leathery hides that haunt mountainous heights; fierce berserker-like aggression in young males during mating season.

name: KRUG
description: A warped Trink counterlineage of squat tool-users, trap-makers, and spiteful scavenger-crafters with a taste for ruin.

name: KRULMAW
description: A powerful feline ambusher of tallgrass and brush, known for speed and force.

name: LAMASUMERA
description: A fearsome chimera of the open plains with a lion's body, horned majesty, and a predator's cunning; dreaded for sudden violence and impossible form.

name: LARK
description: A swift, keen-eyed songbird of field and grove, often trained to carry correspondence across great distances.

name: LEAPER
description: A smaller leaping frog with powerful hind legs, fly trap mouth, and a habit of attacking from brush, broken stone, or uneven ground. Usually dangerous in pairs or packs rather than alone.

name: LIQUID GRAFTWRIGHT
description: A rare and highly dangerous variant of the Graftwright that can take on a semi-liquid form, allowing it to infiltrate even the smallest cracks and crevices. Its attacks are more unpredictable and difficult to defend against.

name: LITHOPHANT
description: A massive stone-skinned grazer whose migrations shape trails and clearings.

name: LLEWELL
description: Chaotic Evil Fire Wyrm of Lilith’iel.

name: LONGSPOT SKY-NECK
description: A colossal pterosaur with a giraffe-long neck that browses high leaves and swallows smaller animals whole when opportunity presents; strikes downward from ledges without fully committing its body to danger. Does not chew. Decides.

name: LONGWRITHE SERPENT-WHALE
description: A sixty-foot whale with serpentine motion; not interested in people, which is the only reason people still cross its waters. A surfacing Longwrithe can capsize small vessels without noticing.

name: LYONOL
description: A majestic six-legged feline predator with a mane of coarse quill-like fur that bristles with static energy; pounces with the weight of a lion and the shocking speed of a lightning strike.

name: LYRWISTAG
description: A majestic forest stag with luminous glowing antlers; serves as a silent guardian of Sylvan Grove's deepest secrets.

name: MAL’AKH
description: A fallen Seraphan counterpart of false light, merciless judgment, and divine authority stripped of compassion.

name: MANTICORE
description: A proud leonine beast with barbed tail and keen cunning; often found as a fierce but honorable guardian of roads, courts, and caravans.

name: MARROWJAW BONE-CRUNCHER
description: A night-roving scavenger-predator with jaws shaped like iron bolt-cutters; leaves almost no evidence after feeding. A battlefield visited by Marrowjaws may be mistaken for a clean burial ground until one notices the absence of bones.

name: MARSH CROCODILIAN
description: A large armored ambush predator of swamp waterways. Patient, powerful, and capable of explosive speed over short distances. Rolls prey to disorient before dragging under.

name: MARSH LEECH (GIANT)
description: A forearm-length blood-feeding worm that attaches painlessly. Commonly encountered when wading through still water.

name: MARSH TURTLE
description: A large freshwater turtle of muddy banks and slow rivers. Docile unless cornered. Their shells are used in Matekwan ceremonial percussion.

name: MASSIF OGRE
description: Mountain. A colossal crag-fleshed titan whose stone-like skin is indistinguishable from cliff faces; triggers devastating rockfalls before stepping out to crush intruders.

name: MAULWASP
description: A colossal winged predator of ridge and wildwood, feared for venom and fury.

name: MAWLOAM
description: See Woodland entry. // DIREFETCH - Direfetch Fae, Direfetch Fae are stored on the face of a Fetchcoin, which a Direwarden will use to summon them. They are small, winged, and often mischievous creatures that can be used for various purposes, such as scouting, delivering messages, or even minor combat assistance. -

name: MEREPLATE MAW
description: A smaller but more aggressive lake Ironplate Maw with green-black armor that claims submerged ruins as territory.

name: MERESICKLE SHRIMP
description: A freshwater lake arthropod with broad greenish sickle-claws; a hazard to fishermen cleaning nets by hand in weedy still water.

name: MINE RAUK
description: Underground. A coal-black sleek-feathered rauk that navigates lightless veins of the earth; used by miners as a living indicator of oxygen levels and impending tunnel collapses.

name: MINOTAUR
description: A hulking bull-headed hunter with immense strength, sharp instincts, and a territorial nature.

name: MIRE OGRE
description: Wastes/Marsh. A massive muck-encrusted giant with waterlogged mossy skin; lurks beneath the surface of the marsh, waiting to drag unsuspecting prey into the suffocating depths of the bog.

name: MIRRORLAKE SERPENT-SWAN
description: The most revered strain, found in clear mountain lakes with silver-appearing feathers at sunrise.

name: MISTWOLF
description: A marsh and river predator that dissolves into fog between attacks, rising from still water to drag victims under while they are still conscious.

name: MOLOCHEAN OPERATIVES
description: Agents of the Molochean Hand. Covert, patient. Some appear as allies until they are not. Medium-to-High threat. Campaign-level if the party has drawn the Hand's specific attention.

name: MOLT RAUK
description: Volcanic. A heat-resistant soot-feathered rauk that thrives amidst volcanic vents; a grim omen when it circles low over areas where the earth is about to rupture and bleed magma.

name: MOLTEN GHOSTCRAB
description: A heat-adapted ghostcrab with a black glass shell veined by dull orange glow. Skitters across cooled lava fields and volcanic shorelines, hiding beneath brittle crust before erupting upward.

name: MOONMARROW GOLEM
description: Lunar-aligned construct of moonstone, silver dust, ivory, and reflective lacquer. Its form subtly shifts with moonlight, becoming thin, ghostlike, or crowned with pale radiance.

name: MOSQUITO HAWK
description: A swift aerial predator with the body and talons of a hawk combined with membranous wings and a blood-draining proboscis. Hunts marshes, jungles, and humid lowlands in aggressive feeding swarms.

name: MOSSBACK BOULDER-WOMBAT
description: A squat, heavily built beast that carries green moss, mushrooms, and seedlings in its fur. Druids sometimes follow them to locate healthy old-growth soil.

name: MOSSLING
description: A small moss-cloaked woodland creature that scurries through root and stone; known for shy curiosity, nimble root tendrils, and tending forgotten groves.

name: MOTHWOLF
description: A silent nocturnal predator with pale dusted fur, moth-like wing structures, and a soft powder-coated thorax. The scales shed from its wings interfere with low-level resonance perception and disorient prey in darkness. Hunts primarily at dusk and beneath dense canopy cover.

name: MOUNTAIN GHOSTCRAB
description: A strange high-altitude crab found in mineral springs, wet cave mouths, and mist-heavy cliffs. Its shell mimics stone and lichen, making it almost impossible to spot until it moves.

name: MOUNTAIN GOAT (SOTERIAN)
description: A sure-footed high-altitude grazer capable of navigating terrain that defeats most other creatures. Domesticated variants are used as pack animals in Durinak mountain holds.

name: MOUNTAIN HARE
description: A white-furred hare of high-altitude terrain. Fast, agile, and a primary food source for mountain predators. Its coloring shifts with the season.

name: MUDKING DREAD-CROC
description: A river-bend Dread-Croc coated in silt, reeds, and leeches; dominates ferry crossings and vanishes in brown water.

name: MUDRAK
description: A slippery iridescent amphibian; hard to catch but the primary food source for the marsh's larger aggressive inhabitants.

name: MURKHARTHOMEL
description: A colossal deep-sea cephalobeast with crushing jaws, boned tentacles, and reef-like camouflage; feared for dragging ships into the abyss.

name: MYCID
description: A sentient fungal creature of root and spore, known for quiet patience and a shared communal mind. NOTE: The Verdant Spiral cult (Campaign 3) mirrors Mycid communal mind behavior in its Sporebound corrupted citizens.

name: NÐHÖRH
description: The Cursed; Titan Leviathan.

name: NEREIGILL
description: A dark Nazari counterlineage tied to drowned trenches, black water, predatory currents, and abyssal hunger.

name: NETTLEKID
description: A childlike woodland trickster that never seems to age, known for pranks and fleeting laughter in the trees.

name: NIDH
description: The Dreamweaver; Titan Basilisk.

name: NOCRAT
description: A nocturnal owl-rat of unusual size and cunning, known for chewing through packs, ration crates, leather straps, and exposed wiring. Swarms are a serious threat to sleeping camps and isolated rail outposts.

name: NOCTARYNN
description: A dark, astral-skinned bloodline found across many races and creatures. Noctaryn-born are often unable to speak, leaving them isolated, feared, and easily controlled by stronger factions. Their strength, silence, and violent conditioning have made them prized as hunters, assassins, and shock troops. The Othrod have used them most brutally, leading thousands of Noctaryn into battle against rival clans. //Khell lineages

name: NYX'RAXIS
description: A colossal serpentine Void entity formed from living shadow and fractured resonance. Its body appears partially intangible, coiling through darkness like liquid smoke while crimson eyes burn within its shifting silhouette. Associated heavily with Khoneus worship and regions experiencing severe Veyline corruption.

name: OAKEN WOODSPIDER
description: An arachnid with a wooden body and branch-like legs that blends into forests.

name: OGRE
description: A massive Terraxian counterform warped into brutal adaptive megafauna, using terrain, strength, and endurance to crush intruders.

name: ONI
description: A towering Helianth counterform marked by demonic appetite, horned brutality, and predatory delight in fear.

name: OPHIDIAN HAND
description: A deeply unsettling serpent-bodied predator ending in two red humanoid palms fused at the wrist. At the center of the palms rests an eye that splits open into a feeding mouth when prey is seized. Believed connected to early Void contamination events or failed Darkweaving experiments.

name: ORC RAUK
description: Wastes. A medium-sized sleek-feathered rauk with deep moss-green plumage that reflects the damp shadows of ancient forest.

name: ORRILLACONDA
description: A massive nightmare hybrid featuring the powerful upper torso of a silverback gorilla fused to the immense muscular coils of a titanoboa; can swing through canopy or constrict prey with terrifying speed. - ORILlACONDACHNID - a massive nightmare hybrid featuring the upper torso of a silverback gorilla with five additional arms spawning from it's spine on either side, making it eleven arms and the coil of a terrifying titanboa. The additional arms are covered in a chitinous exoskeleton and end in razor-sharp claws, allowing it to grapple multiple prey simultaneously while constricting with its powerful coils. It is an apex predator of the jungle, feared for its relentless hunting tactics and ability to take down large prey with ease.

name: OSSYRENTH
description: The Malevolent; Titan Minotaur.

name: OTHRITAUR
description: A towering twelve-foot-tall hybrid of a hellmount horse and an orc; muscular armored behemoth that charges through volcanic ash, designed for high-impact shock warfare.

name: OYAMMOTH
description: A towering prairie elephant with sweeping tusks and thunderous gait.

name: PAINTED GRAVEBEAK
description: A smaller island strain of the hammer-billed flightless predator with bright island plumage; more territorial than mainland strains, especially during nesting season.

name: PANOPTES
description: The All-Seeing Horror; Titan Beholder.

name: PEAK RAUK
description: Mountain. A high-altitude slate-plumed rauk that rides powerful thermals; viewed by climbers as a sentinel marking the safest passage through treacherous heights.

name: PEARLHAVEN SERPENT-SWAN
description: A pearl-white island strain whose nesting pair is considered a sign that waters are clean and fish will return.

name: PETALMAW
description: A large deceptive floral predator whose open jaws resemble layered blossoms. Waits among dense flowers or root-choked groves, releasing a sweet scent before snapping shut on birds, small beasts, or careless hands.

name: PIXIE
description: Small fey-like woodland entities with luminous wings and mischievous temperaments. While often playful and curious, Pixies possess a manipulative streak and delight in leading travelers astray through illusion, false lights, and altered paths. Their behavior ranges from harmless mockery to dangerous cruelty depending on mood and territory.

name: PLAINS GIRAFFE (SOTERIAN)
description: A towering long-necked grazer of open savannah. Non-aggressive. A kick from an adult is lethal. Herds of twelve to twenty common near watering sources.

name: PLAINS HORSE (SOTERIAN)
description: The common wild horse of Soteria's open country. Herd-living, fast, and difficult to approach without proper training. Domesticated variants are the primary mount across most of the continent. Feral herds are legally protected in Veridoran territory.

name: PLAINS HOUND
description: A lean, long-legged dog breed developed by Brunar traders for coursing prey across open ground. Loyal, fast, and poorly suited for cold weather.

name: PLAINS VULTURE
description: A large carrion bird that circles dying creatures and fresh battlefields. Non-aggressive unless feeding is interrupted. Their presence in numbers reliably indicates recent death within a half-mile radius.

name: PLATEWYRME ARMOR-CRAWLER
description: A long, many-legged arthropod covered in overlapping plates as hard as worked armor; reactive rather than aggressive, lashing out when disturbed during molting or torchlight exposure. Even dead segments can twitch for hours.

name: POLAR STALKER
description: A massive white-furred predator of the arctic ice flats. The largest natural land predator in Soteria outside the ogre family. Hunts alone. Extraordinarily patient; will follow prey for days before attacking.

name: PRAIRIEHOLLOW
description: Grassland. A grass-armored plains guardian that crushes intruders.

name: PRIDE LION (SOTERIAN)
description: Larger than natural lions, with a resonance sensitivity that makes them instinctively avoid Dybuk-touched areas. Prides of eight to twelve. Apex predators of open savannah.

name: PRISM-WING
description: A small feathered saurian with layered feathers that shift color in sunlight; prized in noble aviaries across Soteria, a rare specimen can be worth more than a trained horse. When frightened, erupts into sudden color and sound to confuse predators.

name: PROSOPON
description: A child-sized masked humanoid horror whose face is never seen directly. Mimics gestures, voices, and expressions stolen from others, often appearing first as someone familiar before the mask's false emotion begins to crack. - PROSPON ACOLYTE - A smaller, more agile version of the Prosopon that serves as a scout and harasser for larger Prosopon. It can mimic voices and gestures but is less convincing than the main Prosopon, making it more likely to be detected before it strikes.

name: PROVINCIAL GUARD
description: The legal military authority of a given province or city-state. Heavily armed. Backed by infrastructure. High threat if fully mobilized against the party.

name: QUAGGA
description: A subspecies of plains zebra with distinctive stripes on its front fading into solid chestnut; primary hardy grazer for the great predators of the grasslands.

name: RAGWOLD
description: A rag-wrapped doll-shaped horror that drags itself through abandoned homes, nurseries, and plague houses. It steals warmth from rooms before attacking, leaving victims stiff, silent, and wrapped in their own torn bedding.

name: RAIL PIRATES
description: Organized criminal groups that target Veinrunner freight. More sophisticated than road brigands — they have inside information and timing knowledge. Medium threat.

name: RAIL WRAITH
description: A Draethic resonance entity that has formed along a section of collapsed or abandoned Veinrunner track where a significant death occurred. Causes disorientation, auditory hallucinations of train sounds, and increasing Dybuk vulnerability in those who linger. Resonance: Draethic.

name: RAVENEIR
description: A towering raven-hawk humanoid with layered slate-black feathers, scaled talons, a hooked executioner’s beak, and crescent arm-blades. Raveneirs haunt ruined temples, tomb roads, and old stone gates, standing as silent wardens until trespassers cross too far.

name: RAZORGALE PTEROSAUR
description: A lean blade-fast pterosaur with cartilage-reinforced wings that cut rough wind; hunts by rising high then folding into steep descents. When the sky goes briefly silent, look up.

name: RAZORPACK WOLF
description: A grimrite-saturated wolf whose bones have partially externalized through its hide as jagged mineral spurs. Hunts in packs of three to five. Resonance: Grimrite-Saturated. Found near old mine collapse sites.

name: RECLUSE WISKARI
description: Soft blue-beige fur, bright orange markings, enormous black eyes, feather-like antennae between tall bat-like ears.

name: REED HERON
description: A tall wading bird of marsh and fen. Non-aggressive. Considered a good omen in Kul'Kal Rakhar territory where their presence indicates clean water nearby.

name: REED-SAIL SKINK
description: Features blue-green markings and a narrow sail. It dives when threatened and can cling to submerged roots to hide.

name: REEDBACK SHAGGYSWINE
description: An oily, water-resistant marsh strain of Shaggyswine that forages along riverbanks for shellfish, reeds, and buried eggs. Smells terrible when wet but nearly impossible to chill.

name: REEDCOIL SERPENT-SWAN
description: A tan-and-green river strain less shy than lake variants; sometimes follows boats upstream.

name: REEDNEEDLE WASP
description: A green-black river strain of Steelneedle Wasp; fishermen string bells near docks to detect its wing-hum before it strikes.

name: REEF GHOSTCRAB
description: A semi-translucent coastal crab that flickers in and out of sight among tide pools, coral shelves, and wreckage-strewn shallows. Ambushes fish, seabirds, and careless hands with sudden snapping claws.

name: REEF SHARK (SOTERIAN)
description: A large, highly territorial shark of coastal reef systems. Attacks boats and swimmers in its territory. Pack behavior around blood or distress in water.

name: REEFSHELL GLACIER-TURTLE
description: A Shellglacier Turtle that grows coral gardens and bright anemones; fish shelter around it as though it were a moving reef.

name: REEFWATCH SKY-NECK
description: A brighter-mottled island Sky-Neck with broad sea-wind wings; nests on white cliffs and feeds on fish, seabirds, crabs, and anything small enough to vanish in one gulp.

name: REEFWHEEL SAWSPIRAL
description: A patterned short-bodied island Sawspiral that hunts reef shelves and wedges itself into coral gaps after prey.

name: REVENANT
description: A death-marked being retaining fragments of purpose, anger, memory, or unfinished obligation.

name: RHIME OGRE
description: Arctic. A hulking rime-crusted giant whose flesh is as hard as glacial ice; immense strength and ability to endure the most brutal sub-zero temperatures.

name: RHINALLON
description: A six-limbed behemoth; four legs for sprinting and charging, two upper arms for grasping and forest-clearing.

name: RHINDLE
description: A small antlered woodland creature known for skittish movements and uncanny silence.

name: RILIK
description: A small cat-like creature known for good luck and fate.

name: RIMEJAW MARROWCRUNCHER
description: A thick-furred tundra Bone-Cruncher that caches frozen bones beneath snow and returns to the same buried carcass for months.

name: RIMEWILD
description: A pale grassland beast known for swift footing, sharp senses, and a cold wary nature.

name: RIMEWING RAZORGALE
description: A pale, narrow-bodied arctic pterosaur nearly invisible against winter skies; wing edges carry frost that glitters just before a dive.

name: RISEN
description: The most common undead. A body reanimated by proximity to a strong Echoing resonance source or Void contamination. No intelligence. No memory. Driven by a residual resonance impression. Found near battlefield sites, mass graves, abandoned settlements, and collapsed Veyline zones. Low-to-Medium threat individually. The problem is scale — they cluster. Resonance: Echoing.

name: RIVER CRAB
description: Common freshwater crustacean. Edible. Pinches. Found under river rocks and along muddy banks.

name: RIVER DOLPHIN
description: An intelligent freshwater cetacean of Soteria's major river systems. Known to assist drowning travelers. Some Nazari communities maintain working relationships with river dolphin pods.

name: RIVER OTTER (SOTERIAN)
description: Large, playful, and surprisingly territorial. Lutra'v Pa'morph consider them distant kin.

name: RIVER PIKE (GIANT)
description: An enormous freshwater predatory fish of deep river channels. Large specimens capable of capsizing small boats.

name: RIVER SERPENT
description: A massive non-venomous constrictor of slow-moving rivers and lake margins. Ambushes from underwater. Lengths of six to twelve feet are common. Campaign-level specimens approaching twenty feet have been reported.

name: ROAD BRIGANDS
description: Opportunistic criminals operating along trade roads and rail access routes. Lightly armed. Motivated by profit. Will negotiate if cornered and outmatched. Low-to-Medium threat.

name: ROCKPIP
description: A small feathered burrower of the high passes with a hooked beak, digging claws, and dense alpine plumage. It hoards vast stores of dried vegetation in its tunnels and is regarded as a sign of good luck by Durinak miners.

name: ROOF CAT
description: A lean, semi-feral cat population that maintains itself on Ashendell's rooftops and warehouse districts. Feeds on tunnel rats and dock birds. Considered good luck by dock workers.

name: ROOST ROC
description: A colossal sky predator feared for its talons and massive beak.

name: ROOT ROC
description: A large bird-like creature with roots for talons that uproots trees in search of prey.

name: ROTA
description: Lawful Good Cosmic Omni Dragon; the celestial Omni Dragon whose aurora-like wings span the heavens.

name: SABLE-SAND SPIRALHORN
description: A desert drill-rhino with cracked sand-colored hide and a darkened polished horn; charges can collapse old sinkholes beneath itself and everything nearby.

name: SAFFRONHIDE PACKRAPTOR
description: A dry-country Packraptor variant with yellow-orange hide that blends into sunburnt grass and late-season plains. Hunts in loose packs using stillness and sudden bursts of speed.

name: SALTJAW MARROWCRUNCHER
description: A pale, lean, long-legged desert Bone-Cruncher that follows exhausted caravans and digs up shallow graves after sandstorms.

name: SALTMAW DREAD-CROC
description: A coastal saltwater Dread-Croc that patrols mangroves, reefs, and harbor mouths; feared more than sharks because it learns boat routes.

name: SAND MOUSE
description: A small nocturnal rodent of the desert floor. Extremely fast. Primary prey for desert predators. Known to carry water reserves in specialized internal pouches.

name: SAND RAUK
description: Desert. A desert-dwelling tawny-plumed rauk that glides over shifting dunes; a silent observer and omen of hidden oases or approaching sandstorms.

name: SAND WYRM
description: A massive subterranean predator of deep desert. Detects vibration through sandy ground. Surfaces explosively to engulf prey whole. The Brunar trade routes account for known Sand Wyrm territories in their scheduling. High threat. Campaign-adjacent in large specimens. - DESERT SCORPION - a sand scorpion that is known most for the feathers that can be seen on its stinger tail.

name: SANDHIDE GREAT-LURK
description: A twenty-foot desert monitor that buries itself beneath loose sand with only nostrils and eyes exposed; venom turns shallow bites into slow disasters.

name: SAPPHIRESCALE PACKRAPTOR
description: A subaquatic Packraptor variant with blue reflective scales that shimmer like moving water. Hunts near reeds, flooded trails, and shallow crossings where prey is slowed by unstable footing.

name: SAVANNAH ZEBRA
description: A striped herd grazer of open country. Skittish and fast. Primary prey for large savannah predators.

name: SAWSPIRAL SHARK
description: A deep-sea shark with a circular whorl of serrated teeth in the lower jaw like a living saw; evolved to cut armored mollusks but known as a boat-killer that grinds along hulls until the sea comes aboard.

name: SCALEBACK LIGER
description: A powerful plains predator with leonine frame and scaled hide along its spine; feared for speed, strength, and relentless pursuit.

name: SEA SERPENT
description: A massive ocean-going serpentine predator. Surfaces rarely. Responsible for numerous ship disappearances in deep water. Distinguished from the Murkharthomel by its lack of tentacles and preference for open-water hunting.

name: SEA TURTLE (GIANT)
description: A vast, ancient sea turtle. Some specimens are old enough to have developed minor spirit-bound resonance. Considered sacred in coastal Nazari communities. Their shells occasionally serve as temporary islands for shipwrecked sailors.

name: SERENWEN
description: Lawful Good Glacial Dragon of Paravel.

name: SEWER HORROR
description: A pale, eyeless predator adapted to the water systems beneath large Soterian cities. Hunts by sound and chemical sense. Capable of emerging through drain access points. The Ashendell City Authority officially denies their existence.

name: SHADOWMOTE
description: A small, shadowy creature that moves in the dark, known for its ability to blend into the background. Attempts to possess the minds of those it attacks.

name: SHARDBACK WOLF
description: A badlands and coastal predator with a hide studded in razor-sharp mineral shards that detach on impact, turning every charge into a spray of living shrapnel.

name: SHARDBOAR
description: A boar with crystalline tusks that can shatter stone.

name: SHELL GLACIER-TURTLE
description: A sea turtle the size of a small house whose shell grows coral, barnacles, reef plants, or layered ice; a surfacing specimen can overturn fishing boats, a diving one can drag anchors and dock chains into the deep.

name: SHORE CROCODILIAN
description: A smaller coastal variant of the marsh crocodilian. Known to pursue prey onto dry land for short distances.

name: SLEET CUR
description: A tundra and arctic hunter whose ice-crusted coat and freezing breath blend into blizzards, wearing down prey with relentless cold and sleet before the final strike.

name: SMILODON
description: Saber-toothed apex predators of the tundra. Hooved, enormous, and relentless.

name: SNOW HARE
description: A white-furred hare of the tundra. Extremely fast. Primary prey for most tundra predators. Their burrow systems are used by travelers as emergency shelter in blizzards.

name: SOVEREIGN MILITARY
description: The armed forces of the Sovereign Kingdom. Professional soldiers with standardized equipment and organized command. High threat.

name: SPARK MOTH
description: A small moth attracted to Grid discharge points and active electrical infrastructure. In large swarms near active Grid nodes their collective wing interference can disrupt sensitive instruments and telegraph equipment.

name: SPHINX
description: A cunning lion-bodied guardian of sunslit ruins, known for riddles, patience, and sudden violence. Found wherever ancient ruins exist.

name: SPHYNX
description: A cunning lion-bodied guardian of sunslit ruins, known for riddles, patience, and sudden violence.

name: SPIRALHORN DRILL-RHINO
description: A colossal grazing beast with a massive spiral horn used to bore into frozen ground, clay banks, root beds, and mineral deposits; charges twist and throw rather than simply impale. Prospectors follow old Spiralhorn trails hoping to find unearthed metals or crystal seams.

name: SPORE BEETLE
description: A slow-moving armored insect that releases defensive spore clouds when threatened. Not dangerous alone. In groups of ten or more the spore concentration causes disorientation and respiratory distress.

name: SPOREFELL OGRE
description: Fungal Forest. A hulking fungal brute that spreads choking spores and rot with every lumbering step.

name: SPOREWALKER
description: A passive fungal biped that shuffles through spore-heavy groves tending older growth. Not intelligent in any recognizable way but responds to distress signals from the Mycid communal network.

name: SPRITE
description: A cruel lesser Fae that weaponizes beauty, tricks, stolen names, and tiny acts of malice into lasting harm.

name: STALACTITE HORROR
description: A predator that clings motionless to cave ceilings disguised as natural rock formations. Drops onto prey below. Its grip prevents normal escape once contact is made. Low threat individually, extremely dangerous in groups.

name: STEELNEEDLE WASP
description: An eagle-sized ancient dragonfly with a metallic exoskeleton and a hardened proboscis capable of punching through leather and mail gaps; drawn to heat, blood, standing water, and bright movement. The whining hum is often worse than the strike.

name: STEPPE OGRE
description: Savannah. A suns-baked nomadic giant with incredible stamina; doesn't sprint but jogs tirelessly until prey collapses from exhaustion and heat.

name: STILLMERE DREAD-CROC
description: A broader, darker lake Dread-Croc infamous for striking from beneath perfectly calm water in flooded ruins.

name: STILLSTONE GOLEM
description: Still-aligned construct of cold stone, frost-veined metal, and unmoving dust. The air around it becomes muted and slow, turning motion into effort.

name: STONE EAGLE
description: A large raptor of the high peaks with talons strong enough to grip sheer rock faces. Non-aggressive toward creatures it cannot carry. Their feathers are valuable trade items.

name: STORMHAWK
description: A large raptor that hunts during electrical storms, riding lightning-charged updrafts with unusual physical tolerance for electrical discharge. Attacks are preceded by the smell of ozone.

name: STORMRIB RAZORGALE
