description: A dark-membrane tundra pterosaur that hunts before storms; its calls are often mistaken for wind tearing through rigging.

name: SUMMIT WARG
description: A massive high-peak titan whose howls echo like avalanches, driving prey off cliffs and narrow ledges with cold, calculating pack tactics.

name: SUMP CRAWLER
description: A large pale predator that inhabits the drainage sumps beneath active grimrite processing facilities. Feeds on waste organic material and anything that falls in. Eyeless. Chemically sensitive. Extremely aggressive when its sump is disturbed.

name: SUNBLADDER CLOUD-VULTURE
description: A pale-winged desert Cloud-Vulture that follows caravans, knowing drought will eventually choose someone.

name: SUNSAIL SKINK
description: A small desert and island reptile with a bright fan-shaped sail that flushes with color to signal temperature, stress, courtship, or warning; used by travelers as a living weather sign. A frightened Sunsail sheds musk that ruins clothes for days.

name: SUNSBACK ORANGUTAN
description: A broad-shouldered grassland ape with suns-warmed fur and immense strength; known for wary intelligence and dominance over scrub and tallgrass.

name: SUNWROUGHT GOLEM
description: Solar-aligned construct of gold-bronze plates and sun-dial markings. Its core brightens in the presence of deception, corruption, or broken oaths.

name: SWAMP RAUK
description: Marsh/Swamp. A black-feathered marsh rauk that drifts low over still water and reedbeds; feared as a grim sign by those who know the fen.

name: SWAMP SNAKE
description: A semi-aquatic constrictor of shallow fen water. Mildly venomous. Bites cause localized inflammation and minor disorientation.

name: TALLSTRIDE TITAN-HOPPER
description: A towering single-toed leaping grazer with the hind strength of a kangaroo and the long watchful face of a deer; when cornered, a kick can cave shields or launch a wolf across a ravine. Nomads follow herds as guides to clean water and stable ground.

name: TAWNYROOT SHAGGYSWINE
description: Possesses shorter tan wool and stronger legs, making it better suited for open country. Common among caravan farmers and agrarian communities.

name: THALORAK
description: A towering crystalline giant whose body consists of immense multi-hued mineral formations threaded with natural resonance channels. Slow-moving and seemingly passive. Some cultures believe they are living geological memory-keepers of Soteria itself.

name: THE ALDERMORE GIANT
description: See Veldt Ogre entry. Distinguished from ordinary Veldt Ogres by scale and apparent age. Has not moved from its known territory in recorded history. The territory has changed around it three times.

name: THE GREY ONE
description: A smokey grey shoulder-up silhouette bearing the form of a classic bearded wizard beneath a vast old-world hat. His body is half-presence, half-vapor, dissolving into cold mist and pale runic fragments that drift through the air like broken geometry. His face is mostly hidden beneath shadow, beard, and brim, giving him the ancient gravity of a sage who has stepped beyond ordinary mortality. He fuses traditional wizardry with a cold, geometric, Witness-like ethereal presence: silent, architectural, distant, and unnaturally precise. Often encountered in dark cave biomes lit by ghostly fungi or faint subterranean glow, he appears less summoned than discovered, as though the cave itself has exposed a wound between realms. The Grey One's soul is now tied to FUBIN, a realm not yet understood. Whether FUBIN is an afterlife, prison, engine, dream-plane, or unfinished world remains unknown. Since the binding, he no longer exists entirely in one place; his body smokes, fractures, and reforms, while the symbols around him may be pieces of his soul leaking through from that strange realm. Threat Level: Unknown / Campaign-adjacent. Resonance: Echoing, Glyphic, Fubin-Bound.

name: THE MURKHARTHOMEL OF THE SUNKEN ROAD
description: A specific Murkharthomel that has claimed the underwater channel between two major port cities as its territory. Named in Nazari shipping records as the primary cause of the Sunken Road being considered impassable. Campaign-level threat. Several expeditions have attempted to clear it. None have reported back definitively.

name: THE PATIENT ONE
description: A Grimrite Wolf of extraordinary age and size whose pack has inhabited the Corren Mountain Mine collapse zone for generations. Has reached a state of crystalline calm. Does not rush. Does not waste movement. Has killed eleven documented expeditions and is believed to be waiting for something specific, though no scholar has survived long enough to determine what.

name: THE WHISPERING FROSTWRAITH OF THE FALORN FJORDS
description: A near-Titan scale Frostwraith that has inhabited the Falorn Fjords for longer than Ködan oral history extends. Does not hunt in the conventional sense — its presence simply makes the region lethal. Titan-adjacent threat. Do not deploy without full campaign context.

name: THICKET MOUSE
description: An unremarkable small rodent found throughout woodland Soteria. Mentioned because players ask about them constantly and they are genuinely everywhere.

name: THISTLEWING
description: A woodland rabbit with winged ears and a penchant for gliding short distances between trees. Often seen flitting between branches.

name: THORN FOX
description: A lean rust-colored fox with barbed guard hairs along its spine. Hunts small game and occasionally pilfers from camp supplies. Clever enough to unlatch poorly secured packs.

name: THORNBACK BEETLE
description: A sturdy heat-tempered insect with a heavily armored spiked shell; can launch razor-sharp thorns like projectiles to impale predators at distance.

name: THORNNEEDLE WASP
description: A bronze and bark-colored forest strain of Steelneedle Wasp that perches on trunks and darts through gaps in dense trees with impossible precision.

name: THREADBOUND GOLEM
description: Loom-aligned construct woven from bone needles, brass spindles, knotted cord, hair, silk, and fate-scripted thread. It binds, pulls, and repositions enemies as if combat itself were a pattern to be corrected.

name: TIDAL WRAITH
description: A semi-translucent aquatic predator of coastal tidal zones. Feeds on the distress resonance of drowning victims. Encourages panic rather than attacking directly. Resonance: Void-Tainted.

name: TIDEGLASS GOLEM
description: Water-aligned construct made from blue-green glass, river stones, coral, and suspended water. Its body bends and reforms like a current, allowing it to absorb blows and force enemies into drowning grips.

name: TIDEGLASS PRISM-WING
description: A pearl, teal, and coral island strain of Prism-Wing; favored as court gifts but becomes melancholy if kept away from sea wind.

name: TIDEHIDE GREAT-LURK
description: A darker, stronger-swimming island monitor that raids nests, beaches, and fishing camps with patient precision; more tolerant of saltwater than inland strains.

name: TIDEHOOK SCYTHE-SHRIMP
description: A red-brown-black mottled Scythe-Shrimp from tide pools and volcanic lagoons; nearly invisible against wet stone.

name: TIGER-FLY
description: A vibrant insect with tiger-like stripes and razor-sharp mandibles; hunts in swarms capable of stripping a wounded creature to the bone in minutes.

name: TOYTAKER
description: A small predatory horror associated with missing children, broken toys, and rooms that should not be locked from the inside. Imitates play sounds to lure victims closer before striking from cupboards, crawlspaces, or beneath beds.

name: TRACKBIRD
description: A small bird that has built its ecological niche around the Veinrunner rail network. Nests in signal towers and beneath platform overhangs. Feeds on insects disturbed by train passage. Their sudden absence from a rail station is considered a bad omen by experienced conductors.

name: TRIDENT TAILED LYNX
description: A silver-furred lynx that can turn nearly invisible in shadow; its large tail splits into three.

name: TROLL OF AETHER
description: A pale radiant troll marked by faint star-like scars, luminous veins, and an unsettling nearness to spirit places. It appears near shrines, high ruins, old sanctums, and thin places where the mortal world brushes the divine. Its wounds close in flashes of white-gold light, though profane iron, Nullite, and broken rites can interrupt its restoration.

name: TROLL OF AIR
description: A gaunt, long-limbed troll with hollow bones, wind-torn skin, and a chest that bellows like a storm vent. It hunts cliffs, high bridges, broken towers, and mountain passes, using sudden gusts and thunderous roars to unbalance prey. Its regeneration weakens in sealed spaces where the air is still and heavy.

name: TROLL OF EARTH
description: A broad stone-backed troll with soil-packed hide, root-tangled limbs, and mineral plates across its shoulders. It sleeps half-buried in hillsides, cairns, old roads, and battlefields, rising when footsteps disturb the ground above it. Its regeneration is slow but extremely difficult to stop, knitting flesh with clay, root, and buried bone.

name: TROLL OF FIRE
description: A heat-scarred troll with coal-dark hide, ember-lit wounds, and a furnace glow deep in its throat. It haunts burned forests, forgefields, volcanic roads, and ruined battle camps, feeding on charred meat, hot metal, and anything foolish enough to approach its lair. Its regeneration is aggressive, but cold water, smothering ash, and deprivation of air can slow the healing.

name: TROLL OF LOOM
description: A strange troll threaded with cordlike growths, scar-patterns, and webbed strands of pale connective tissue. It is drawn to crossroads, old family holdings, oath-sites, and places where many lives have become entangled. Its regeneration follows patterns; if one wound is repeated exactly, the second cut may reopen the first.

name: TROLL OF LUNAR
description: A moon-pale troll with reflective eyes, tide-marked skin, and a habit of appearing where dreams, sleep, and hidden paths overlap. It hunts at night along shorelines, silver marshes, old roads, and ruins touched by moonlight. Its body heals strongest under the moon, but becomes sluggish beneath harsh noon sun.

name: TROLL OF SOLAR
description: A golden-eyed troll with cracked sunburned hide, bright scars, and a roar that sounds like stone splitting under heat. It favors open plains, temple roads, dry mesas, and exposed ruins where daylight gives it strength. Its regeneration is fastest in direct sun, but it weakens in deep shade, sealed caves, or prolonged darkness.

name: TROLL OF SPARK
description: A restless twitching troll with bright nerves visible under the skin, snapping teeth, and sudden bursts of violent speed. It haunts workshops, storm-struck trees, ruined laboratories, and places where invention or wild life-force has gone wrong. Its regeneration is erratic, sometimes healing instantly and sometimes mutating the wound into something worse.

name: TROLL OF STILL
description: A deathly quiet troll with waxen skin, slow breath, and glassy eyes that rarely blink. It waits motionless in frozen ponds, sealed crypts, silent ruins, and abandoned halls, sometimes mistaken for a statue or corpse until prey comes close. Its regeneration is slow but preserves it through starvation, cold, and wounds that should have ended it long ago.

name: TROLL OF VOID
description: A black-skinned troll whose outline seems to drink lamplight and silence nearby sound. It haunts abandoned wells, collapsed underroads, dead caverns, and places where fires refuse to stay lit. Its regeneration is unnatural and partial, with missing flesh returning as cold shadow before becoming meat again.

name: TROLL OF WATER
description: A slick blue-gray troll adapted to rivers, marshes, flood tunnels, and cold underground lakes. It waits beneath bridges, reeds, and black water, dragging prey under rather than killing quickly. Its flesh closes like wet clay, and old specimens are said to remember every voice they have drowned.

name: TROLL OF WEIGHT
description: A massive low-slung troll with dense bones, sagging stone-thick flesh, and footsteps that make loose rock tremble. It dwells in deep valleys, pressure caverns, collapsed forts, and places burdened by old oaths or heavy grief. Its presence makes armor feel heavier and running feel harder, while its own body resists being moved, lifted, or knocked aside.

name: TUNDRA OX
description: A massive shaggy-coated bovine of the frozen north. Domesticated by Terraxian and Ködan communities as a draft animal and food source. Their wool is among the warmest natural fiber available in Soteria.

name: TUNDRA WOLF
description: Larger than the woodland grey wolf, adapted to extreme cold, with pale grey-white coats. Packs of up to twenty. Extremely coordinated hunters. Known to follow Smilodon kills.

name: TUNNEL MOLE
description: A large burrowing creature used by Durinak engineering teams to locate soft soil pockets before drilling. Blind, docile, and extremely difficult to startle. Considered good luck in mining culture.

name: TUNNEL RAT
description: Aggressive, large, disease-carrying rodent of city sewer and cellar systems. Found everywhere. Considered the most common hazard in Ashendell's lower districts.

name: TWILLWISP
description: A small luminous woodland creature that drifts through brush and branch like a living lantern.

name: TYPHON
description: The Destroyer; Titan Demon.

name: UNICORN
description: A rare horned creature of untouched glades, tied to calm and purity.

name: VALVE-WHALE
description: A massive gas-filled cephalopod that drifts through chemical clouds, filtering toxins and occasionally providing "islands" of buoyant safety for weary travelers.

name: VEINSPINNER
description: A mechanized arachnid that weaves intricate webs of glowing threads. Formidable in combat, using its mechanical limbs to strike with precision and power.

name: VEK'AR
description: A small humanoid figure with glowing eyes found in the Altum (Heaven planes).

name: VELDT OGRE / THE ALDERMORE GIANT
description: Grassland/Plains. A massive monolithic titan with weathered stone-like skin cracked by the relentless glare of the three suns; a jagged sentinel over scorched open country.

name: VENOMHIDE GREAT-LURK
description: A twenty-foot monitor lizard with venom that turns shallow bites into slow disasters; strikes once, withdraws, and follows wounded prey until venom, infection, and exhaustion do the work. "The ground is listening" means a Great-Lurk is nearby.

name: VESSEL
description: A Dybuk-Host in late stage who has been in that state long enough to develop independent goals beyond simple feeding. The original host was someone significant enough that their memory and the Dybuk's hunger have merged into something new. Can pass as human in dim light. Campaign-level threat.

name: VESUVIAN PACKRAPTOR
description: A heat-hardened Packraptor variant with dark basalt scales and ember-colored throat markings. Its packs drive prey toward unstable ground, ash clouds, and choking smoke.

name: VOID RAUK
description: Cosmic/Void. A gaunt shadow-slicked rauk with pitch-black plumage that absorbs all light; a soul-chilling omen that circles over those marked for consumption by the Void.

name: VOLTAIC GOLEM
description: Spark-aligned construct of copper, iron, glass coils, magnetized plates, and storm-captured lightning. Its movements are sharp, twitching, and violently sudden.

name: VORLANTHAR
description: A massive beetle-like predator protected by a heavily armored carapace and crushing mandibles capable of splintering timber and bone alike. Drawn toward vibration; erupts violently from beneath loose earth when disturbed.

name: VYRLYPH
description: A twisted Tel’ari offshoot whose elegant form has become unstable, backward, and wrong beneath corrupted resonance.

name: WASTE HYENA
description: A lean, heavily built scavenger adapted to chemical waste zones. Immune to most contact toxins. Hunts in packs and has been known to follow industrial work crews waiting for accidents.

name: WASTELAND GAROON
description: A heavy-backed scavenger-beast adapted to chemical flats and ruined scrublands. Its cracked hide resists toxins, and it follows the scent of carrion, leaking machinery, and wounded travelers across long distances.

name: WATER STRIDER
description: A large aquatic insect that walks on the surface tension of still water. Completely harmless. Their sudden departure from a water surface indicates something large moving beneath.

name: WENDIGO
description: A gaunt antlered predator of woods, driven by endless hunger and savage cunning.

name: WHIPSICKLE SCYTHE-SHRIMP
description: A three-foot ancient arthropod with two spiked whip-like appendages; a single one is dangerous to swimmers, a feeding cluster is a butcher's storm in the water.

name: WHITE-RUNE APEX-URUS
description: A pale tundra strain of the great Runebear with black claws and a roar that carries for miles over snow; stalks caribou, mammoth-calves, and unwary sled teams.

name: WHITECOIL SERPENT-SWAN
description: A long-necked aquatic reptile clothed in pale feathering along the neck and upper body; peaceful and graceful but strikes with its neck like a whip when nesting. Its presence is considered fortunate in lakeside cultures.

name: WHYLL
description: A beautiful woodland lure that sings travelers off the path to be devoured.

name: WOODHOLLOW
description: Forest. A bark-armored forest guardian that crushes intruders in sacred groves.

name: WOOLSNOUT SHAGGYSWINE
description: A stout, wool-covered domestic beast with a soft trunk-like snout for rooting through soil and snow; bonds strongly to households, remembers voices and locked cupboards, and screams loud enough to wake a hamlet when threatened. Common among northern herders and traveling families.

name: WRAILL LEAPER
description: A cursed frog-like bramble beast that hops through the undergrowth, its croaking calls echoing in the darkness.

name: WRETCH
description: A gaunt, hairless city creature that subsists on refuse and waste heat from industrial districts. Not intelligent. Not aggressive unless cornered. Their presence in large concentrations indicates significant food waste or organic decay below a structure.

name: WUID
description: A tiny feral woodland creature known for sudden speed, sharp instincts, and mischievous cruelty.
